title: Hyperspeed
description: Three.js warp-tunnel effect with line segments and car lights racing through a procedural road. Hold to accelerate into warp speed. Ships with 6 preset configurations.
prompt_text:
# Hyperspeed

Three.js warp-tunnel effect with line segments and car lights racing through a procedural road. Hold to accelerate into warp speed. Ships with 6 preset configurations.

## Use when
- Setting a dramatic, sci-fi atmosphere on a dark landing page hero.
- Triggering a cinematic first-load reveal with speed-up mechanics.
- Showcasing a product with an energetic, forward-motion background.

## Stack
- Framework: react
- Libraries: three, framer-motion

## Install
```bash
npm install three framer-motion
```

## Component — hyperspeed.tsx
```tsx
// Source: animations_007.md
// Exports: Hyperspeed (default), hyperspeedPresets
// Props: effectOptions (HyperspeedOptions), className
// HyperspeedOptions: distortion, length, roadWidth, islandWidth, lanesPerRoad,
//   fov, fovSpeedUp, speedUp, colors (roadColor, islandColor, background,
//   shoulderLines, brokenLines, leftCars, rightCars, sticks), onSpeedUp, onSlowDown
// 6 presets: turbulentPreset, mountainPreset, xyPreset, longRacePreset, deepPreset, staticPreset
// See full source in raw/animations_007.md
```

## Notes
- Component source is ~600 lines. See `raw/animations_007.md` for full verbatim code.
- Requires Three.js (>600 KB). Lazy-load for performance.
- Press-and-hold triggers warp speed via `onSpeedUp`/`onSlowDown` callbacks.
